title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 18th March 2026 | TV5 Money Live

recommendations.note: Stop averaging down; mid-cap IT outlook weak
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao said headwinds in IT are clearly high and Persistent and Coforge are among the weakest on the weak side. He advised the caller (who was averaging down at a 20-30% loss) to stop adding for now, saying averaging only on a 5-10% price fall is wrong; reduce exposure into any dead-cat-bounce / intermittent rally. The business-model re-invention and AI-adoption story will decide the stock, which may stay range-bound for now.

recommendations.note: Stop averaging down; mid-cap IT outlook weak
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao grouped Coforge with Persistent (and LTTS) as mid-cap IT names where doubts about the business model are being expressed. He said this is not the time to add and one should cut exposure on any rally. The stocks may stay range-bound; how the companies re-invent/transform and adapt AI by next year is the key.

recommendations.note: Wait and watch; not a bad stock but temporarily weak
recommendations.detail: On the EMS/financial-infra names, Kutumba Rao said Cams and Dixon are the two weak ones; once aggressively bought by institutions, institutional interest has now fallen. He told the caller to stop trying to add for now and wait-and-watch, calling them not bad companies but going through a temporarily bad phase.

recommendations.note: Wait and watch; EMS supply-chain fears
recommendations.detail: Kutumba Rao said EMS companies face fears that the logistics chain will be upset, and a weakening economy from the crude crisis could indirectly hit sales of fast-moving electrical goods, with Dixon especially exposed. As of now he advised wait-and-watch, saying it is not a bad stock but in a temporarily bad phase; do not average down.

recommendations.note: Wait and watch; check if institutional exit or a bottom
recommendations.detail: On Cyient DLM (the defence/aerospace-focused EMS name), Kutumba Rao noted interesting volume action the previous day - over 3 crore shares traded, far above recent times - yet the stock still fell around 2.5% after that volume. He said one should watch whether this marks a bottom or whether it was an institutional exit before acting.

recommendations.note: Accumulate for medium term; promoters buying
recommendations.detail: For NCC, Suzlon and Vimta Labs, Kutumba Rao advised accumulating all three with a medium-term perspective. On NCC specifically he noted promoters have been buying on and off / continuously. (His Zoom audio was breaking up; the anchor relayed that the gist heard was 'all three can be bought / accumulated for the medium term'.)

Kutumba Rao grouped Suzlon with NCC and Vimta Labs as medium-term accumulate ideas. (Said while his Zoom audio was breaking up; the anchor confirmed the accumulate verdict was attributed to Kutumba Rao.)

Kutumba Rao included Vimta Labs among the three (with NCC and Suzlon) he advised accumulating with a medium-term view. (Said with audio trouble; the anchor relayed the accumulate verdict as Kutumba Rao's.)

recommendations.note: Hold; only realty stock in PSU space, good dividend
recommendations.detail: A caller holding NBCC at Rs 107 asked whether to continue. Kutumba Rao said hold/continue - it is the only realty stock among PSUs, no doubt about that. It corrected with recent problems along with others, but has good dividend, consistent business, and recently new payments and fresh work orders have come; the caller's cost price will easily be recovered at some point.

recommendations.note: Stay away from precious metals for now
recommendations.detail: In his market view Kutumba Rao said gold has fallen below 5,000 dollars an ounce on higher dollar prices, and speculators warn that if it stays below 5,000 a sharp correction of 600-700 dollars could come over the next 2-3 days. As of now he advised staying away from gold and precious metals; no need to chase it as a 'safe haven'.
